column2: Grand Destroyers are destroyers with bigger main armament, either in calibre or in the number of guns. In reality only a few nations build such ships. Their heavier armament reduced their seaworthiness. You can use them as a cheap alternative to light cruisers.

Destroyers carry a mixture of light guns and torpedoes for use against other surface ships. Researching improved weapons will mean we can build new destroyer models which are more effective against surface targets.

Destroyers are the fastest ships in the fleet, but very short-ranged. Their speed gives them much flexibility. However, it's important our destroyers maintain their speed advantage. More powerful engines will mean we can build faster, longer-ranged destroyers.

Destroyers have lightly-built hulls with very little armour protection. The strength of the hull and the quality of its design affect how easy our destroyers are to sink. Better protection let us build destroyers which are more durable in combat.

These vessels were basically small destroyers with the armament focus on the torpedo weapon. Their other armament were some small main guns (in the most cases 10,5cm). In terms of speed they were as fast as the destroyers or even faster. In WWII they were mostly used as escorts or submarine hunters. They can be used as an alternative to destroyers, but their fighting abilities are very limited.
Torpedo Boats carry a mixture of very light guns and torpedoes for use against other surface ships.

Torpedo Boats are fast ships. Their speed gives them much flexibility against other surface ships. However, it's important our Torpedo Boats maintain their speed advantage. More powerful engines will mean we can build faster, longer-ranged Torpedo Boats.

Torpedo Boats have lightly-built hulls with very little armour protection. The strength of the hull and the quality of its design affect how easy our Torpedo Boats are to sink. Better protection let us build Torpedo Boats which are more durable in combat.

The Deutschland class was a series of three Panzerschiffe, a form of heavily armed cruiser, built by the German Reichsmarine more or less in accordance with restrictions imposed by the Treaty of Versailles. The British began referring to the vessels as pocket battleships, in reference to the heavy firepower of the relatively small vessels.

The principal feature of the Pocket battleships design was that it had guns of large enough calibre —280 mm (11 inches)— to out-gun almost any enemy cruiser fast enough to catch it, while being fast enough to outrun almost any enemy powerful enough to sink it.

Pocket Battleships are quite fast and long-ranged warships. The adoption of an all-diesel propulsion system was a radical innovation at the time and contributed to significant savings in weight. This in turn increased operating range and speed. However, it's important our heavy cruisers don't get left behind by newer ships. More powerful engines will mean we can build faster, longer-ranged ships.
Pocket Battleships have armour which can stand up against anything apart from a battleship shell. Thicker and stronger armour plate will mean we can build more durable models.
Heavy guns, better guns, firing heavier shells at a higher speed, will mean we can build capital ships which are even more powerful against surface targets.
Our battleships risk being left behind by enemy warships. Improving our battleship engine designs will mean we can build battleships which steam faster.

Battleships are protected by heavy steel plate. Improved armour schemes have thicker plates, stronger alloys, or better layouts. Researching them means we can build battleships which are more difficult to sink.

The coastal submarines were small ships with a small crew, only little number of torpedoes and especially only a short range. If your submarine force only operates in smaller sea area, then you can use them instead of the more expensive normal ones.
Submarines carry small anti-aircraft guns. By improving these guns, we can refit our submarines and give them better protection against air attack

Submarines are quite slow and short-ranged. By researching better diesel engines for use on the surface and better electric motors for use underwater, we will be able to build submarines with more speed and endurance.

To escape attack, submarines must dive underwater. The stronger the hull of a submarine is, the deeper it can dive, and the more likely it is to survive a depth-charge attack. If we improve our submarine hull designs we will be able to build submarines which are more resistant to damage.

Frigates are small ships, normally bigger than a corvette, and are specialized in submarine hunting. They have the same capability as destroyers in ASW warfare but are smaller and much less capable surface combatants.

Supercarrier is an unofficial descriptive term for the largest type of aircraft carrier, usually displacing over 70,000 long tons. Supercarriers are the largest warships ever built. They can carry 3 CAG units.

The development of the anti-aircraft cruiser began in 1935 when the Royal Navy re-armed HMS Coventry and HMS Curlew. Torpedo tubes and 6-inch (15 cm) low-angle guns were removed from these WWI light cruisers and replaced by ten 4-inch (10 cm) high-angle guns with appropriate fire-control equipment to provide larger warships with protection against high-altitude bombers.
Researching and building better transports will allow for greater sea invasion capabilities
The amount of aircraft was limited by the size of the ships hangars. However by optimising the flight deck space on a carrier more aircraft could be carried.
Naval anti-aircraft warfare, is any method of engaging hostile military aircraft in defence of naval forces. Better naval heavy machine guns's will improve our ability to destroy enemy aircraft.
Naval anti-aircraft warfare, is any method of engaging hostile military aircraft in defence of naval forces. Better naval cannon will improve our ability to destroy enemy aircraft.
By adapting our capital ship's secondary guns and smaller ships primary guns for high angle fire we can use them to counter threats from the air with increasing deadliness.
More powerful engines and bigger ships will mean we can build longer-ranged transport in order to let them cross the oceans.

The HMS Illustrious class were the first carriers to incorporate their flight decks into the superstructure. This meant the flight deck, rather than the lower hangar deck was armoured, leading to much better resistance to aerial attack.
Secondary Guns. The secondary armament of a capital ship are smaller, faster-firing weapons that are typically effective at a shorter range than the main (heavy) weapons.
A fast battleship utilizes the speed of a battlecruiser without sacrificing armour or armament. These ships can keep up with a carrier battlegroup.

Germany's Type XXI was the first submarine designed to operate primarily underwater. This was a radical departure from all previous sub classes with a hull designed best for operating on the surface. The subs can operate submerged longer, quieter, and faster than any before.
A submarine snorkel is a device which allows a submarine to operate submerged while still taking in air from above the surface. This will allow our subs to be less vulnerable to attack when cruising.
By towing loud noise making devices behind our merchant vessels, they will become less vulnerable to acoustically guided torpedoes.
